title: Headers
intro: Section headers carve detail panels, drawers, and widgets into scannable bands. Three live on a tinted full-width band (DataSectionHeader, DataSectionValueHeader, EditSectionHeader); two are lightweight widget titles with an underline flair (UnderlineHeader, WidgetHeader). Pick by job, not by looks.

blocks.body:
Reach for a header component whenever a panel or drawer holds more than one cluster of fields — the tinted band is the visual contract that says "new section starts here." `DataSectionHeader` is the plain label band; `DataSectionValueHeader` adds a headline metric to the band; `EditSectionHeader` adds an edit/save toggle that drives a view↔edit mode for the fields below it. `UnderlineHeader` and `WidgetHeader` are a different species: no band, just an uppercase title with a short SVG underline, meant for dashboard widgets and cards — `WidgetHeader` is `UnderlineHeader` plus a right column for controls and a bottom border.

All five render their title uppercase at 600 weight in `--gray-600`. Never hand-roll an uppercase `<div>` to fake a section break — the band components keep spacing, casing, and theme tint consistent across every detail surface.

blocks.title: Which header
blocks.intro: One decision: what does the section need beyond a label?

rows.name: DataSectionHeader
rows.when: Plain labeled band — read-only section, no metric, no actions.

rows.name: DataSectionValueHeader
rows.when: Band that leads with a headline number (totals, averages). Value renders 1.8em bold in `--theme-option`.

rows.name: EditSectionHeader
rows.when: Section whose fields toggle between read-only and editable. The header owns the pencil/save toggle.

rows.name: UnderlineHeader
rows.when: Widget or card title with the underline flair, nothing else.

rows.name: WidgetHeader
rows.when: Widget title plus right-aligned controls, with a bottom border separating header from widget body.
rows.code: <WidgetHeader title='Volume' controls={<DateSkipper startDate={date} daysToSkip={1} onChange={onDateChange} />} alignControls='right' />

rows.name: SearchGridHeader
rows.when: Never. Deprecated and flagged for removal — it still uses antd `visible` APIs. Use `GridControlBar` and GraviGrid's own header slots for grid pages.

blocks.title: DataSectionHeader
blocks.caption: The plain band: full-width tint from --theme-color-2-dim, 1.2em padding, title forced uppercase at 600 weight in --gray-600.

blocks.title: DataSectionValueHeader
blocks.caption: Same band, two halves: title in the left antd Col (span 12), headline value in the right — 1.8em bold in --theme-option. The value column starts at the 50% mark; it is not right-aligned.

rows.notes: Section label. `DataSectionHeader` types it as `string`; `DataSectionValueHeader` accepts any node. CSS uppercases it either way — write it in normal case.
DataSectionValueHeader only. The headline metric. Money is decimal dollars — `$0.0100/gal`, never cents. The title beside it carries an inline `marginTop: '4%'` to sit on the value's baseline.
Appended to `detail-section-header` on the outer antd Col. Both components span 24 — the band always fills its container.

blocks.title: EditSectionHeader — view state
blocks.caption: View state: title band with the EditSaveButton pencil right-aligned (title Col span 18, button Col span 6). With editing={true} the pencil swaps to a save icon that submits the enclosing antd Form.

blocks.title: EditSectionHeader
blocks.intro: Verified against source — the props are `editing` and `onEdit`. There is no `isEditing`, `onSave`, or `onCancel`; passing them does nothing and the header stays in view state.

rows.notes: Drives the EditSaveButton: false renders a pencil that fires `onEdit`; true renders a save icon with `htmlType='submit'` — it submits the nearest enclosing antd Form.
Fires on pencil click. Flip your `editing` state here. Exiting edit mode happens in the Form's `onFinish` — there is no `onSave` prop.
Adds the `.success` class: band turns `--gray-300` with white bold text. Used as a saved/confirmed flash on the section.
Moves the toggle button next to the title instead of the far right, and renders `children` in the remaining Row space — the slot for extra header controls.
Title Col width, only honored when `leftAlignToggle` is true. Default layout is fixed at 18/6.

blocks.title: UnderlineHeader
blocks.caption: Uppercase widget title with the WidgetUnderline SVG flair (#002140 navy, 41×2) anchored under the first characters. Captured with the title in a positioned wrapper — required, see gotchas.

rows.notes: Widget title, uppercased at 600 weight in `--gray-600`.
Renders the underline in white instead of #002140 — for titles sitting on dark fills.
WidgetHeader only. Right-column slot for pickers, buttons, toggles.
WidgetHeader only. antd Col spans for the two halves — must sum to 24.
WidgetHeader only. Passed to the controls Col as `align`; set `'right'` to push controls to the edge.

blocks.title: Canonical section with an editable header
blocks.note: The header must sit inside the Form it saves — the save button is a plain HTML submit, not a callback.

blocks.title: Source CSS values
blocks.intro: From `DataDisplay/Headers/index.css` and `Layout/Widgets/index.css` in the library source.

rows.use: Band background for all `.detail-section-header` components. Swings green↔blue with the active theme — never assume a fixed hue.
Band inset on all four sides.
Title styling shared by every band header.
WidgetHeader row; the border separates header from widget body.
Underline flair; absolutely positioned at left 0 / top 1.5em of the nearest positioned ancestor.

items.why: Those props don't exist — React drops them silently and the header never leaves view state. The save path is the enclosing Form's onFinish.

items.do: Wrap EditSectionHeader and its fields in one antd `<Form onFinish={...}>`.
items.dont: Wire saving through a click handler on the header.
items.why: The save icon is `htmlType='submit'` — outside a Form it submits nothing and the click is a no-op.

items.do: Use DataSectionHeader for every section break in a panel or drawer.
items.dont: Hand-roll uppercase `<div>` labels with custom backgrounds.
items.why: The band carries the theme tint and consistent 1.2em rhythm; ad-hoc labels drift on both.

items.do: Write money values in decimal dollars — `$0.0100/gal`.
items.dont: Use cents (¢) anywhere in a value header.
items.why: Gravitate copy standard: decimal dollars across all surfaces.

items.do: Build grid pages with `GridControlBar` and GraviGrid's header slots.
items.why: It's deprecated, flagged for removal, and built on antd APIs (`visible`, `dropdownClassName`) that are themselves deprecated.

items.title: The published package ships without header CSS
items.detail: excalibrr 5.2.1's `dist/index.css` contains none of the `.detail-section-*` or `.widget-*` rules — the components mount unstyled (no band, no uppercase, inline underline). The specimens above were captured with the library-source CSS applied. Before building on these components, verify the styles exist in your build; if not, port `DataDisplay/Headers/index.css` and `Layout/Widgets/index.css` into the app until the package build is fixed.

items.title: `editing`, not `isEditing`
items.detail: EditSectionHeader's mode prop is `editing`. The demo showcase itself passes `isEditing`/`onSave`/`onCancel` — which is why both of its specimens render the pencil. Wrong prop names fail silently; the header just never shows the save state.

items.title: Save is a form submit, not a callback
items.detail: In edit state the EditSaveButton renders with `htmlType='submit'`. It only works inside an antd Form; exit edit mode in `onFinish`. There is no cancel affordance — if the section needs one, render it via `leftAlignToggle` + `children`.

items.title: WidgetUnderline needs a positioned ancestor
items.detail: The underline SVG is `position: absolute; left: 0; top: 1.5em`. If no ancestor establishes a positioning context, it anchors to the page and escapes the header entirely. Give the title an `inline-block`/`relative` wrapper (production usage puts it inside a positioned Col).

items.title: DataSectionValueHeader's value is not right-aligned
items.detail: The value sits in the right half-Col and starts at the 50% mark, which reads as roughly centered on wide bands. Pass a styled node as `value` if the layout needs edge alignment.
